h2: The Role of an MP
h3: If you cannot find the support you need here, please contact me providing as much information as possible.

Latest News Local Campaigns Erdington High Street HMOs Fly tipping Conker Island Park Kingstadning Pride in Place Survey APPGs APPG on Black Health APPG on Less Survivable Cancers APPG on Hospice and End of Life Care In Parliament Help and Advice Need Advice or Support? Emergency Support Contact Me Office Standards Privacy statement More Menu Help and Advice اردو | বাংলা | عربى | Soomaali Polskie | Română | Kurdish The Role of an MP My role as an MP is to represent everybody in the Birmingham Erdington constituency in Westminster. An MP can assist with matters for which Parliament or central Government is responsible for. There is also help available here: Homelessness If you or someone you know is homeless or at risk of becoming homeless, then it is important you speak to Birmingham City Council as soon as possible. If you need emergency accommodation now and you are aged 25 or over or are aged 18 to 25 and have dependent children, phone the council on: 0121 303 7410 option 3. Phone lines are open: Monday to Friday, 9:00am to 5:00pm. Under 25s If you are under the age of 25 and have no children, you will need to attend St Basils Youth Hub instead. You can phone them on: 0300 303 0099 . The council’s Domestic Abuse Homelessness Hub, in partnership with Cranstoun , can help people escaping domestic abuse find a safe place to stay. If you need their help, you can call them on: 0121 272 8905 , or email: [email protected] . To find out what documentation you need to speed up your request, click this link here: https://www.birmingham.gov.uk/info/20207/homelessness/1196/i_need_accommodation_now Out of hours Support: If you are aged 18 or over, and you need support to find accommodation outside office hours or over the weekend, call the council on: 0121 303 2296 . If you are under the age of 18, call us on: 0121 675 4806 . Other Help Other Helpful Organisations can be found on the council’s website here: https://www.birmingham.gov.uk/info/20207/homelessness/1216/homeless_advice Foodbanks I can’t afford food If you cannot afford to buy food I can provide you with a food voucher, please contact my office on 0121 306 4275 or [email protected] . Or alternatively, you can contact Citizen’s Advice at 08082787990 who can refer you to a foodbank. Birmingham City council also have online referral form that you can fill in: https://www.birmingham.gov.uk/xfp/form/825 The Active Wellbeing Society have put together a map with the latest list of Trussell Trust foodbanks, independent foodbanks and pantries, community cafes and cooked food providers, on street food outreach and additional (non food related) support. You can take a look at what is in your local area here: https://theaws.co.uk/food-provision-database/ Domestic Abuse If you are in immediate danger, phone 999, or get someone else to phone, and state whether you need the police, ambulance or fire service. Move to a safe place, avoiding kitchens or stairs, and stay near a door for a quick escape. If in doubt, get out. You can find a more in depth list of support here: https://birmingham.gov.uk/info/50047/crime_and_safety/2823/get_help_for_victims_and_survivors_of_domestic_abuse Birmingham and Solihull Women’s Aid Birmingham and Solihull Women’s Aid is the main organisation that provides services for women experiencing domestic abuse in Bimingham and Solihull. www.bswaid.org 0808 800 0028 – everyday, 9:15am – 5:15pm Cranstoun Help adults and young people facing difficulties with domestic abuse, alcohol, drugs, housing and criminal justice. [email protected] www.cranstoun.org Monday to Friday 9:00am to 5:00pm: 0121 633 1750 Men’s Advice Line help to understand rights and options and to support to access refuge for male victims. www.mensadviceline.org.uk Monday to Friday 10:00am to 8:00pm: 0808 801 0327 (free
